# TESS Program G08119

Title: Bridging Asteroseismology And Gyrochronology With Wide Binaries.

PI: Lares Martiz, Mariel - Embry-Riddle Aeronautical University

Type: SMALL

Summary: This proposal aims to bridge asteroseismology and gyrochronology as independent age determination methods by using Wide Binary (WB) systems where at least one component is pulsating, and one or both exhibit rotational modulation. The coeval property of WB components provides age cross-checks, exposing the limitations of the methods when disagreeing. Comparing these independent age diagnostics, we will set important constraints on how stellar structure changes as stars evolve, i.e., recalibrating activity-age relations. The extended coverage in TESS Cycle 8 will improve the precision of detecting stellar rotation, pulsations, and flares, especially for older stars with longer periods. The project's expected impact is a robust assessment of the rotation-mass-activity-age relation.
